## What is GeoServer?

- [**Open-source geospatial data server**]{.orange}
- Publishes raster & vector data via [**OGC standards**]{.blue2}:
  - [**WMS**]{.blue2} (Web Map Service) — rendered map tiles
  - [**WFS**]{.blue2} (Web Feature Service) — raw vector features
- Layers are directly consumable by [**web apps**]{.blue2}

::: {.notes}
**GeoServer** is an open-source geospatial data server. It enables the publishing, storage, and serving of geographic data (rasters, vectors) via standardized protocols such as **WMS** (Web Map Service) or **WFS** (Web Feature Service). This makes it possible to distribute map layers that can be consumed directly by web applications.
:::

## Deploying GeoServer on Onyxia

- [**Not in the Onyxia service catalogue**]{.orange} — but deployable on the underlying Kubernetes cluster
- Requirements:
  - VSCode service with [**Kubernetes admin**]{.blue2} role
  - Define `Deployment`, `Service`, `Ingress` resources manually
- Once deployed: accessible via a [**cluster Ingress URL**]{.blue2} like any other service

*Demo:* [GeoServer interface — example WMS layer](https://geoserver-satellite-images.lab.sspcloud.fr/geoserver/web/?0)

::: {.notes}
GeoServer is **not part of the Onyxia service catalog**, but it is entirely possible to deploy it manually on the underlying Kubernetes cluster. This requires a VSCode service with the Kubernetes **admin** role, as well as familiarity with Kubernetes resources: `Deployment`, `Service`, and `Ingress`. I will not go into that level of detail here, but rest assured that the operation is feasible.
:::

## Pipeline → GeoServer Integration

- Inference pipeline [**automatically pushes results**]{.orange} to GeoServer on completion:
  - Land cover and Building change [**polygons**]{.blue2} → new WFS layers
- GeoServer creates and [**versions the layers**]{.blue2} automatically

::: {.notes}
Our inference pipeline is **directly connected to our GeoServer**: with each execution, the predicted land cover and detected changes in built structures are automatically pushed to the GeoServer, which creates and updates the associated geographic layers.
:::
